[PDF_PAGE 58 | score=6] Mummy, Bog CLIMATE/TERRAIN: Swamp If only damaged, the bog mummy may retreat to a bog. After FREQUENCY: Rare resting in the bog for two full days, an injured bog mummy ORGANIZATION: Solitary begins to rejuvenate at a rate of 6 hit points per hour. ACTIVITY CYCLE: Any Unlike other mummies, it retains moisture and thus is not at DIET: None all affected by normal fire. Even magical fire inflicts only half INTELLIGENCE: Low (5-7) damage upon a bog mummy, It is instead vulnerable to cold, TREASURE: Nil which can cause ice crystals to grow inside its tissues causing its ALIGNMENT: Chaotic evil body to rupture, Cold-based spells thus inflict double the normal amount of damage. NO.APPEARING: 1 A bog

Creatures completely drained of CLIMATE/TERRAI Any land Any land Strength by lesser shadowraths die, and such a death is irreversible, FREQUENCY: Very rare Very rare even with a wish. Strength points return at the rate of one per hour. ORGANIZATION: Solitary Solitary Lesser shadowraths are immune to the effects of normal and silver ACTIVITY CYCLE: Any Any weapons, and can be turned as ghasts. DIET: Nil Nil Lesser shadowraths are created by the Crown of Horns. [PDF_PAGE 72 | score=4] Crown of Horns XP Value: 150,000 GP Value: The Crown of Horns, in its original form, was an electrum helm fully covered with small horns, and a row of black gems around its edge. Since its reformation, the crown is an electrum circlet with four bone horns mounted around its edge and one large black diamond centered over the wearer's brow—while black as obsidian, the stone is clear, and weird energy dances within the faceted gem. While powerful in its first incarnation, the might of the Crown of Horns has only increased in the past decade. Its powers and abilities, derived from the essence of Myrkul (the god of the dead before Cyric, slain in the Godswar), are: * The Crown of Horns surrounds

OF ATTACKS: 2 DAMAGE/ATTACK: 2d4 SPECIAL ATTACKS: Advanced illusion, mass charm, disease SPECIAL DEFENSES: Immune to sleep, charm, hold, poison, and death magic MAGIC RESISTANCE: 10% SIZE: M (5' tall) MORALE: Steady (11-12) XP VALUE: 975 A Ravenloft siren is a hideous, undead creature with the upper body of a woman and the lower body of a fish. Bright light is painful to them—bright day
[PDF_PAGE 36 | score=3] stances), the victim instantly returns to normal size. Casting dispel magic on a tiny death minnow caus- ing it to grow to its monster size of 8 feet, making it a far easier target. There are no known “wild” popu- lations of death min- nows. These are magi- cal guard beasts. As they are created magi- cally, they do not need to breed. In fact, it is uncer- tain if they even have male and female sexes. As a magical guardian beast, the death minnow ensures that the immediate vicinity it is set to guard remains free of such dangerous predators as adventurers. If the party can kill one, its blood might be used as an ingredient in the ink used for penning enlarge or shrink on spell scrolls. If, in
[PDF_PAGE 81 | score=3] Spyder-Fiend deflects most blows. The creature's wolf head is covered with luxuriant fur, and a triple row of sharp-looking spines runs down the back of the neck to the spider body. The fur is black or gray, but the shell and spines can be almost any color and are always vividly marked with swirls, bands, or spots of contrast- ing colors. A raklupis's humanoid arms are knotted with thick cords of muscle and covered with tufts of hair that match the wolf head. Raklupises have even more beautiful and compelling voices than lycosidiliths do; they weigh about 450 pounds. Combat: A raklupis has a fearsome bite attack, Not only do the creature’s powerful jaws inflict significant damage, but its bi
